data.title: No more 'good place'
data.description:
Key points:
  • STOXX 600 up 0.4%
  • Busy earnings day, French banks struggle, Rolls Royce gains
  • ECB and BoE meetings to come
  • U.S. futures steady

NO MORE 'GOOD PLACE'

European Central Bank policymakers might once again soon be performing a balancing act as the economic picture grows increasingly tricky.

Euro zone inflation jumped to 3% in April from 2.6% in March, flash data showed, with energy prices largely driving the increase. On the brighter side, any potential second-round effects from higher energy prices don’t seem to have kicked in yet.

But of course they can’t be excluded. For example food prices may rise due to fertiliser shortages, and the impact of energy prices on headline inflation will likely increase even more, Peter Vanden Houte, chief economist for Belgium, Luxembourg and the Eurozone at ING, noted.

“Inflation is likely to creep higher towards the 4% level,” he said.

Meanwhile, euro zone economic growth was faint in the first quarter, data also showed on Thursday.

That leaves the ECB in a difficult situation.

In fact, the data points “suggest that the ECB has shifted from being 'in a good place' in early 2026 to facing a brutal stagflation shock,” said Claus Vistesen, chief eurozone economist at Pantheon Macroeconomics.

data.title: Valero Energy beats estimates for first-quarter profit
data.description:
U.S. refiner Valero Energy surpassed Wall Street expectations for first-quarter profit on Thursday, helped by higher margins.

The San Antonio, Texas-based company posted an adjusted profit of $4.22 per share for the three months ended March 31, compared with analysts' expectations of $3.16 per share, according to data compiled by LSEG.

data.title: Twenty One Outlines Plan to Acquire Strike and Elektron, Targeting Mining, Finance & Securitization
data.description:
Twenty One outlined an operating strategy centered on potential acquisitions of Strike and Elektron to build integrated Bitcoin financial services and mining infrastructure.

Key Highlights:

  • Announced potential acquisitions of Strike (Bitcoin financial services) and Elektron (large-scale Bitcoin mining) to consolidate capabilities.
  • Strategy focuses on four pillars: financial services & distribution, Bitcoin infrastructure, capital markets, and mergers & acquisitions.
  • Capital markets plan includes future securitization of loan books and mining revenue and using treasury-backed, non-dilutive leverage.
  • Objective is to move beyond treasury holdings to an integrated platform anchored by recurring revenue and long-term Bitcoin accumulation.
  • Presentation materials, press release, and transcript from the CEO's Bitcoin 2026 Conference talk are furnished as exhibits.

Original SEC Filing: Twenty One Capital, Inc. [ XXI ] - 8-K - Apr. 30, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Royal Caribbean Group posts Q1 Adjusted EPS $3.60, Revenues $4.45B and raises full-year Adjusted EPS view
data.description:
Royal Caribbean Group reported first-quarter 2026 results with total revenue of $4.45 billion and Adjusted diluted EPS of $3.60, beating prior guidance. Net income attributable to Royal Caribbean Group was $941 million (diluted EPS $3.48) and Adjusted EBITDA was $1.7 billion. The company updated full-year 2026 Adjusted EPS guidance to $17.10–$17.50 and noted stronger-than-expected demand alongside higher fuel cost assumptions.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue: $4,452 million for the quarter (up 11% year over year).
  • Gross Margin (as reported): Gross Margin $1,744 million; Gross Margin Yields $127.29 per APCD.
  • Operating income: $1,162 million for the quarter.
  • Net income attributable to Royal Caribbean Group: $941 million; Diluted EPS $3.48.
  • Adjusted measures: Adjusted Net Income attributable $975 million; Adjusted diluted EPS $3.60; Adjusted EBITDA $1,702 million.

Business Highlights

  • Strong demand environment: Record WAVE season and continued strength in close-in bookings drove higher pricing and onboard revenue growth.
  • Capacity and guest volumes: First-quarter capacity up 8% year over year, serving 2.5 million guests (12% increase) with APCD of 13.7 million and load factor ~109%.
  • Bookings dynamic: Mediterranean and West Coast of Mexico itineraries moderated in late Q1 due to geopolitical and air-service disruptions but have largely rebounded and are running ahead of last year for remaining inventory.
  • Portfolio expansion and destinations: Launched Royal Beach Club Santorini, expects delivery of Legend of the Seas in Q2, announced orders for Icon VI and Icon VII, and continues expansion of private destinations and the Royal ONE credit card to deepen guest engagement.
  • Liquidity and capital actions: Liquidity of $6.9 billion as of March 31, 2026; issued $2.5 billion of senior notes and repurchased 2.9 million shares for $836 million with $1.0 billion remaining repurchase authorization.

Original SEC Filing: ROYAL CARIBBEAN CRUISES LTD [ RCL ] - 8-K - Apr. 30, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Donegal Group reports Q1 2026: Net income $11.5M, revenue $236.0M, combined ratio 99.8%
data.description:
Donegal Group reported first-quarter 2026 results with total revenues of $236.0 million and net income of $11.5 million, or $0.31 per diluted Class A share. The company reported a GAAP combined ratio of 99.8% (vs. 91.6% year-ago) and non-GAAP operating income of $11.9 million. Book value per share rose to $17.54 at March 31, 2026.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenues: $235.996 million for the three months ended March 31, 2026.
  • Net income: $11.511 million; diluted net income per Class A share: $0.31.
  • Non-GAAP operating income: $11.889 million (after-tax adjustment for investment losses).
  • GAAP combined ratio: 99.8% for Q1 2026, compared with 91.6% for Q1 2025.
  • Book value per share: $17.54 at March 31, 2026 (up from $16.24 at March 31, 2025).

Business Highlights

  • Net premiums earned decreased 4.9% to $221.357 million year-over-year for Q1 2026.
  • Net premiums written declined 3.2% to $239.290 million, driven by a 13.1% decrease in personal lines offset by a 2.2% increase in commercial lines.
  • Commercial lines showed growth in net premiums written (up 2.2%), led by automobile and other commercial categories and solid retention.
  • Personal lines continued to contract (personal net premiums written down 13.1%), with automobile and homeowners declines cited as drivers.
  • Underwriting performance note: elevated weather-related losses ($17.2 million) and higher large fire losses ($12.2 million) increased the loss ratio; core loss ratios for both commercial and personal segments improved modestly versus prior year.

Original SEC Filing: DONEGAL GROUP INC [ DGICA ] - 8-K - Apr. 30, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Carrier Global Corp Q1 Income Drops
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - Carrier Global Corp (CARR) released a profit for first quarter that Drops, from the same period last year

The company's bottom line totaled $239 million, or $0.28 per share. This compares with $412 million, or $0.47 per share, last year.

Excluding items, Carrier Global Corp reported adjusted earnings of $482 million or $0.57 per share for the period.

The company's revenue for the period rose 2.4% to $5.341 billion from $5.218 billion last year.

Carrier Global Corp earnings at a glance (GAAP) :

-Earnings: $239 Mln. vs. $412 Mln. last year.

-EPS: $0.28 vs. $0.47 last year.

-Revenue: $5.341 Bln vs. $5.218 Bln last year.

-Guidance:

Full year EPS guidance: $ 2.80

Full year revenue guidance: $ 22 B

data.title: Brazil's Braskem elects Petrobras CEO as new chair
data.description:
Shareholders of Brazilian petrochemical firm Braskem elected a new board of directors on Wednesday, naming state-run oil company Petrobras' CEO Magda Chambriard as chair, the meeting minutes show.

The move comes after conglomerate Novonor signed a deal last week to sell its controlling stake in Braskem to private equity fund Shine I, controlled by asset manager IG4 Capital.

Petrobras holds 47% of Braskem's voting shares and 36.1% of its total shares.
